name: Diamond pavilion silhouette
description: Overall silhouette of a pavilion on a diamond (45°-rotated square) footprint, presenting points to the cardinal directions and flat faces on the diagonals. The rhombus reads as a jewel-like garden or water pavilion, with a strong central focus and four symmetrical approaches up to each vertex. Interior rooms are rectangular inside the diamond; the four triangular tips are thick wall or planted alcoves. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides the centre.
placement_rules: Footprint is a diamond (square rotated 45°) with vertices to the cardinal directions.
A single rectangular room or hall fills the inscribed centre; the four tips are wall/alcove.
1-2 storeys; the form suits a garden or water pavilion.
Approaches climb to one of the four vertices.
Keep the inscribed room >= 4x4 so it is habitable.

chinese.notes: Garden water-pavilion with upturned eaves meeting at a central finial.

fantasy.notes: Crystalline pavilion with a faceted roof rising to a point.
